_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of five soft commodities including coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice, stands at 141 compared to a 2015 baseline of 100, indicating a roughly 41% increase since 2015. This index reflects global commodity prices and shows a sharp and uneven rise over recent years rather than steady inflation. Not all components have contributed equally to the increase, leading to a varied price dynamic across the basket. The notable jump points to sector-specific issues rather than broad-based trends.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled, rising 99%, and orange juice has increased 34%, the largest drivers behind the overall basket's ascent. Wheat and sugar prices have grown more moderately, by 19% and 13% respectively. These changes reflect underlying weather events and crop diseases, such as citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice, and climatic stresses like frost and drought in arabica coffee growing regions. Soft commodities trade in relatively thin markets, where supply disruptions can significantly sway prices.
notes.why: The headline rise in this basket is primarily driven by a few weather-sensitive crops rather than widespread inflationary pressures. Given these commodities' exposure to environmental factors, price levels can remain elevated for extended periods if adverse conditions persist. This dynamic underscores the volatility inherent in soft commodity markets.
